title: ʔaǰɛčxʷot — "How are you?" · Klahoose, Tla'amin & Homalco territory
summary: Cortes Island is the traditional territory of the Klahoose, Tla'amin (Sliammon) and Homalco First Nations — Northern Coast Salish peoples whose shared language is ʔayʔaǰuθəm. The welcome sign at the Cortes Natural Food Co-op opens with the ʔayʔaǰuθəm greeting ʔaǰɛčxʷot. Cortes is a rainforest island at the northern tip of the Salish Sea.

title: Klahoose First Nation — T'oq (Squirrel Cove)
summary: The Klahoose First Nation's main community on Cortes is at T'oq (Squirrel Cove). Klahoose traditional territory spans Cortes Island and Toba Inlet. The nation operates Klahoose Coastal Adventures and the Klahoose Wilderness Resort in Desolation Sound, sharing culture, language and territory with visitors.

title: Cortes Island Museum & Archives
summary: Local museum in Mansons Landing keeping the island's stories: First Nations heritage, settler history, natural history collections, and a photographic archive of island life.

title: Hollyhock — learning & retreat centre
summary: Canada's well-known lifelong-learning centre on the beach at Highfield, hosting workshops, artists, writers and leadership programs since 1982.

title: CKTZ 89.5 FM — Cortes Community Radio
summary: The island's community radio station, broadcasting local voices, music and news from Mansons Landing — streaming live around the world.

title: CorilAir seaplane — the Vancouver flight
summary: CorilAir's scheduled floatplane leaves the Seair dock at YVR South Terminal at 4:00 pm and works its way up the Discovery Islands — the fast way on and off Cortes (about an hour in the air, from $449 one-way). The southbound run gathers passengers late morning toward the 12:30 Campbell River departure.

title: The Hague–Gunflint community watershed
summary: Hague and Gunflint Lakes form the island's freshwater heart. Water is shed from the surrounding land after rain and snowmelt, traveling through streams and groundwater into the lakes — what happens on the land shapes the health of the water.
